includes: The first external proposed recommendation candidate set.
Proposed, non-actionable candidates referencing stored external candidate Card Facts.
Qualified card references: deck:scryfall:<id> and candidate:scryfall:<id>.
Traceability to baseline pressure points, open questions, project goals, card facts, and functional roles.
excludes: Deck changes, new deck versions, or decision log entries.
Changes to rec-001, card facts, candidate card facts, Knowledge, analysis, decisions, or reports.
Upgrade, cost, or popularity claims of any kind.

explicit_no_recommendations_boundary.statement: Every candidate in this set is proposed only. No candidate is accepted or rejected, no candidate is actionable, and no deck change is authorized by this file. A Product Owner decision is required before any candidate advances, and any future accepted change additionally requires a decision log entry and a new deck version file. The referenced candidate Card Facts remain facts_only and are not recommendations.

reference_conventions.pressure_point_id: Format '<analysis_id>:pressure_point:<index>' where <index> is the zero-based position in the structural_pressure_points array of the referenced analysis file. Example: 'baseline_v1.0:pressure_point:0'.
reference_conventions.open_question_id: Format '<analysis_id>:open_question:<index>' where <index> is the zero-based position in the open_questions array of the referenced analysis file. Example: 'baseline_v1.0:open_question:2'.
reference_conventions.project_goal: Verbatim goal string from project.json 'goals' or brief.json 'improvement_areas'.
reference_conventions.card_reference: Qualified references only. deck:scryfall:<id> resolves against workshop/card-data/cards.json. candidate:scryfall:<id> resolves against workshop/card-data/candidate_cards.json, must not exist in cards.json, and the fact record must remain recommendation_status facts_only. Legacy bare scryfall:<id> is reserved for rec-001 and is not allowed in this set.
reference_conventions.incoming_cards: Cards entering the deck if a candidate is later accepted; candidate:scryfall:<id> references only.
reference_conventions.outgoing_cards: Cards leaving the deck if a candidate is later accepted; deck:scryfall:<id> references only, and each must be a playable v1.0 commander/main-deck card rather than a sideboard card.
reference_conventions.affected_cards: Union traceability field: every card a candidate touches, including all incoming_cards and outgoing_cards references.

candidate_schema.description: Contract for each entry of the 'candidates' array, schema version 1.1. Extends the rec-001 contract with qualified card references and directional fields for deck-changing candidates.

candidate_id.description: Unique ID within the recommendation set. Format 'cand-<zero-padded-number>', for example 'cand-001'.

candidate_type.description: The kind of change the candidate describes. 'knowledge_review_candidate' proposes reviewing Knowledge coverage (for example role tagging) rather than a deck change. 'role_coverage_candidate' describes a role-level gap to address without naming specific cards.

status.description: Lifecycle status. New candidates start as 'proposed'. See candidate_lifecycle for allowed transitions.

source_analysis_id.description: analysis_id of the analysis document that motivated the candidate, for example 'baseline_v1.0'. Every candidate must trace to an existing analysis.

source_pressure_point_ids.description: Pressure point IDs per reference_conventions.pressure_point_id. May be empty only for knowledge_review_candidate entries motivated by open questions alone.

related_project_goals.description: Verbatim project goal strings the candidate serves. At least one is required; candidates serving no stated goal are out of scope.

related_roles.description: role_id values affected by or motivating the candidate. Must exist in role_taxonomy.json.

related_categories.description: category_id values affected by or motivating the candidate. Must exist in role_taxonomy.json.

evidence_summary.description: One-paragraph neutral summary of why this candidate exists, grounded in cited evidence items.

evidence_items.description: Each item: { evidence_type, reference, note }. 'reference' points into the cited source. candidate_card_facts items reference stored external candidate facts via candidate:scryfall:<id>. At least one item is required, and every incoming external card must be backed by at least one candidate_card_facts item.

confidence.description: Curator confidence that the candidate addresses the cited evidence. Not a card quality score.

proposed_change.description: Neutral description of the change being considered. Phrased as a candidate for evaluation, never as an instruction.

affected_cards.description: Every card the candidate touches, as qualified references: deck:scryfall:<id> for cards in workshop/card-data/cards.json and candidate:scryfall:<id> for cards in workshop/card-data/candidate_cards.json. Bare scryfall:<id> references are not allowed in rec-002 or later sets. Must include every reference from incoming_cards and outgoing_cards.

expected_impact.description: Which structural measurements (role counts, category counts, curve, color access) the change is expected to move, and in which direction.

tradeoffs.description: What the deck gives up or risks structurally if the candidate is accepted. May be empty only with reviewer-recorded justification.

risks.description: Failure modes: identity erosion (for example Myr density), goal conflicts, or Knowledge gaps that could invalidate the evidence.

constraints_checked.description: Project constraints verified before proposing, for example commander color identity, Myr typal identity preservation, and format legality per stored card facts.

incoming_cards.description: Cards that would enter the deck if the candidate were later accepted. Must use candidate:scryfall:<id> references. Empty for candidates that do not bring cards in.

outgoing_cards.description: Cards that would leave the deck if the candidate were later accepted. Must use deck:scryfall:<id> references and resolve to playable v1.0 commander/main-deck cards, not sideboard cards. Empty for candidates that do not remove cards.

is_actionable.description: Always false until an explicit user decision is recorded. Candidates are inputs to a decision, not decisions.

requires_user_decision.description: True for every candidate that would change the deck. Only the Product Owner decides.

requires_testing.description: True when acceptance should wait for playtest or simulation evidence.

creates_new_deck_version.description: True when acceptance would produce a new version file. Existing version files are never edited.

decision_log_required.description: True when acceptance or rejection must be recorded in decisions/ before any further action.

reviewer_notes.description: Dated notes from reviewers. Neutral language only.

user_decision.description: The Product Owner's recorded decision, if any. Null until decided.

decision_id.description: ID of the decision log entry in decisions/ once a decision exists. Null until decided.

follow_up_tasks.description: Task references created as a result of review, for example testing or Knowledge review tasks.

rules: Every status change is recorded in reviewer_notes with a date.
'accepted' and 'rejected' are terminal and require a recorded user_decision and decision_id.
'accepted' does not change the deck by itself: any deck change happens only through a new deck version file plus a decision log entry.
'needs_testing' candidates return to 'under_review' only with new playtest_note or simulation_result evidence attached.
No candidate may be created or promoted while this set's status is 'schema_only'.

validation_expectations: Every candidate uses qualified card references; bare scryfall:<id> never appears.
Every candidate:scryfall:<id> reference resolves in candidate_cards.json, does not exist in cards.json, and its fact record is facts_only.
Every deck:scryfall:<id> reference resolves in cards.json.
incoming_cards use candidate:scryfall:<id> only; outgoing_cards use deck:scryfall:<id> only and resolve to playable v1.0 cards.
affected_cards includes every incoming_cards and outgoing_cards reference.
Every incoming card is Commander legal and within the commander color identity per stored candidate facts.
Every candidate is proposed, is_actionable false, user_decision null, decision_id null.
Every deck-altering candidate requires user decision, testing, a decision log, and a new deck version.
Every incoming external card is backed by at least one candidate_card_facts evidence item.
Candidate text contains no cost, popularity, verdict, or scoring language.
This set never modifies rec-001, deck, version, card-data, knowledge, analysis, decision, or report files.

candidates.evidence_summary: The baseline flags colored-cast consistency as unverified: 23 nonland cards have colored costs against 21 colored land sources for a five-color identity. City of Brass and Mana Confluence are stored candidate facts whose oracle text adds one mana of any color, which is the most direct land-slot answer to that pressure point. This candidate reviews adding one or both as a mana-base adjustment.

evidence_items.note: Colored-cast consistency for the five-color identity is unverified.
How often colored nonland cards can be cast on curve is an open simulation question.
Oracle text: taps for one mana of any color; deals 1 damage when it becomes tapped.
Oracle text: taps for one mana of any color at the cost of 1 life.
Mana fixing is a stated Sprint 1 improvement area.

candidates.proposed_change: Candidate for review: introduce City of Brass and/or Mana Confluence to the mana base to increase any-color land sources. No outgoing card is nominated yet; if the Product Owner advances this candidate, selecting which land or lands would leave is part of the review and would form a follow-up swap decision.
candidates.expected_impact: Expected to increase colored_source and fixing_land counts (currently 21 and 12 among 34 lands) and improve access to the 23 colored nonland cards. Does not change nonland role distributions.
tradeoffs: Each card causes incremental damage or life loss when used for colored mana, per stored oracle text.
Any land they would replace has its own current function; the deck's colorless production needs (19 colorless sources) also weigh against removing colorless-producing lands.
Adding lands without removing any is not possible in a fixed 100-card deck, so a later swap target is unavoidable if advanced.
risks: Life loss accumulates in a deck that may also want life as a resource for other effects.
Improving colored access is only useful if colored casting is actually a failure mode; open_question:2 has not been tested or simulated yet.
constraints_checked: Both incoming cards are Commander legal per stored candidate facts legalities.
Both incoming cards have empty color identity, a subset of the commander's five-color identity.
Neither card exists in workshop/card-data/cards.json; both remain facts_only candidate records.
Myr typal identity is unaffected: this candidate touches only the land slot.
No deck, version, decision, or card-data file is changed by this candidate.
reviewer_notes: 2026-07-09: Proposed for Product Owner review as an external candidate.
follow_up_tasks: Color-access simulation for open_question:2 before any decision.
If advanced, a follow-up review to nominate the outgoing land or lands.

candidates.evidence_summary: The baseline shows selection weighted toward artifact tutoring (7 artifact tutors) while card advantage stays small (11 cards). Urza's Saga is a stored candidate fact whose chapters create Construct tokens that scale with artifact count and search the library for a 0 or 1 mana-cost artifact. In a deck with 50 artifacts including Sol Ring, Skullclamp, Lightning Greaves, and six artifact lands, that is a repeatable land-slot selection effect. This candidate reviews adding it.

evidence_items.note: The card advantage layer is comparatively small; land-slot selection adds access without spending a nonland slot.
Chapter abilities: taps for colorless, creates a Construct that scales with artifacts, tutors an artifact with mana value 0 or 1.
The deck holds 50 artifact cards and several 0-1 mana value artifact targets.

candidates.proposed_change: Candidate for review: add Urza's Saga to the mana base as a utility land contributing artifact selection, Construct board presence, and colorless mana. The outgoing slot is left to a later Product Owner review.
candidates.expected_impact: Expected to add utility_land, artifact_tutor, token_producer, and scaling_threat coverage from the land slot, raising selection density without consuming a nonland slot.
tradeoffs: Produces only colorless mana, which sits in tension with the colored-access pressure point this set also addresses.
Sacrifices itself after its final chapter, so the land count effectively drops by one over time.
As an enchantment land it is exposed to enchantment removal that ordinary lands avoid.
risks: Its value depends on the density of 0-1 mana value artifact targets staying high through future deck changes.
Adding another colorless source could worsen colored-cast consistency before cand-007 style fixing is reviewed.
constraints_checked: Commander legal per stored candidate facts legalities.
Empty color identity, a subset of the commander's five-color identity.
Not present in workshop/card-data/cards.json; the fact record remains facts_only.
Myr typal identity is unaffected: this candidate touches only the land slot.
No deck, version, decision, or card-data file is changed by this candidate.
reviewer_notes: 2026-07-09: Proposed for Product Owner review as an external candidate.
follow_up_tasks: Testing to observe chapter timing against the deck's actual curve.
If advanced, nominate the outgoing card in a follow-up review.

candidates.evidence_summary: The baseline notes the deck has no card tagged ritual_or_burst_mana and that its win-condition structure is under-described, with Aetherflux Reservoir as the sole tagged combo payoff. Krark-Clan Ironworks is a stored candidate fact that converts artifact sacrifices into two colorless mana each. With 50 artifacts, token producers, and Myr Retriever already in the deck, it is a burst-mana engine that could feed large turns and the existing payoff. This candidate reviews adding it.

evidence_items.note: Win-condition structure is under-described; burst mana feeding the tagged combo payoff is one route to investigate.
Whether Aetherflux Reservoir has sufficient structural support is an open question.
Oracle text: sacrifice an artifact to add two colorless mana.
The deck currently has zero ritual_or_burst_mana role assignments.

candidates.proposed_change: Candidate for review: add Krark-Clan Ironworks as an artifact sacrifice mana engine for explosive turns. The outgoing slot is left to a later Product Owner review.
candidates.expected_impact: Expected to add the deck's first ritual_or_burst_mana coverage plus a repeatable sacrifice_outlet, and to increase mana_engine density feeding damage payoffs and large commander turns.
tradeoffs: Consumes the deck's own artifacts, which competes with artifact-count payoffs and Myr board presence.
Four mana on a curve that averages 3.0 and peaks at two-mana plays.
risks: Can pull the deck's identity toward a dedicated combo engine, which the Product Owner may not want; this is an identity decision, not a structural one.
May reduce casual readability of game states for other players.
Its strongest lines depend on combo mapping (open_question:0) that has not been done; testing is required to see whether it is an engine or a distraction.
constraints_checked: Commander legal per stored candidate facts legalities.
Empty color identity, a subset of the commander's five-color identity.
Not present in workshop/card-data/cards.json; the fact record remains facts_only.
Myr bodies are a sacrifice resource only if the Product Owner accepts that mode; the candidate itself does not change the Myr identity.
No deck, version, decision, or card-data file is changed by this candidate.
reviewer_notes: 2026-07-09: Proposed for Product Owner review as an external candidate.
follow_up_tasks: Playtesting focused on whether sacrifice lines conflict with Myr board plans.

candidates.evidence_summary: The baseline questions how reliably the commander's Myr-focused triggers find targets given 14 Myr typal cards, and the commander itself creates Myr tokens on every Myr spell. Mana Echoes is a stored candidate fact that adds colorless mana equal to the number of creatures sharing a type whenever a creature enters. In a deck that makes many same-type Myr bodies, that converts typal board presence into burst mana. This candidate reviews adding it as a Myr-identity-aligned explosiveness engine.

evidence_items.note: Myr typal density versus the commander's triggers is untested; this candidate leans into the typal plan rather than away from it.
Oracle text: whenever a creature enters, add colorless mana equal to creatures sharing a creature type with it.
The commander and 13 other Myr typal cards produce or are same-type bodies, including token generators.

candidates.proposed_change: Candidate for review: add Mana Echoes as a Myr typal burst mana engine. The outgoing slot is left to a later Product Owner review.
candidates.expected_impact: Expected to convert Myr token production into colorless burst mana, adding ritual_or_burst_mana and payoff_engine coverage that scales with the typal plan the project wants to preserve.
tradeoffs: Produces colorless mana only, which funds artifacts and the commander's generic costs but not colored spells.
Its {2}{R}{R} cost adds red pips to the colored-cast consistency question the baseline already flags.
risks: Board-dependent: it does little from an empty board, so it can arrive as a late win-more piece rather than acceleration; confidence is low until tested.
An enchantment in a deck whose protection effects are artifact-directed.
constraints_checked: Commander legal per stored candidate facts legalities.
Color identity ['R'] is a subset of the commander's five-color identity.
Not present in workshop/card-data/cards.json; the fact record remains facts_only.
Directly supports the stated Myr identity rather than diluting it.
No deck, version, decision, or card-data file is changed by this candidate.
reviewer_notes: 2026-07-09: Proposed for Product Owner review as an external candidate.
follow_up_tasks: Testing for open_question:3 (Myr trigger frequency) with and without this engine.

candidates.evidence_summary: The baseline shows the deck's selection is already its strongest access layer while raw card advantage stays small. Tezzeret the Seeker is a stored candidate fact whose abilities untap up to two artifacts, search the library for an artifact with mana value X or less and put it onto the battlefield, and provide an artifact-animating emblem-free ultimate. Untapping mana rocks and tutoring artifacts directly to the battlefield overlap with the deck's engine plan. This candidate reviews adding it.

evidence_items.note: Selection is the deck's main access route; battlefield tutoring compresses the path from search to play.
Loyalty abilities: untap up to two artifacts; search for an artifact with mana value X or less onto the battlefield; -5 animates artifacts.
The deck already runs 7 artifact_tutor and 6 tap_untap_engine assignments this card would reinforce.

candidates.proposed_change: Candidate for review: add Tezzeret the Seeker as an artifact tutor and untap engine in the planeswalker slot. The outgoing slot is left to a later Product Owner review.
candidates.expected_impact: Expected to raise artifact_tutor and tap_untap_engine density, put searched artifacts directly onto the battlefield, and add a repeatable untap effect for mana rocks.
tradeoffs: Five mana value on a curve that averages 3.0; it competes with the deck's other five-plus plays.
Reinforces roles the deck already covers well (7 artifact tutors, 6 untap engines) rather than filling an empty role.
risks: A planeswalker in a deck with little evasive pressure or dedicated blockers may not survive a turn cycle.
Its {3}{U}{U} cost adds blue pips to the colored-cast consistency question the baseline already flags.
constraints_checked: Commander legal per stored candidate facts legalities.
Color identity ['U'] is a subset of the commander's five-color identity.
Not present in workshop/card-data/cards.json; the fact record remains facts_only.
Myr typal identity is unaffected; the card supports the artifact engine plan.
No deck, version, decision, or card-data file is changed by this candidate.
reviewer_notes: 2026-07-09: Proposed for Product Owner review as an external candidate.
follow_up_tasks: Testing planeswalker survivability in representative games.
Role-overlap review against existing tutors before any decision.
